headline: Tech stocks worst week in a year, AI momentum breaks
detail: Broad AI enthusiasm has visibly stalled, with financing concerns and competitive pressure hitting sentiment across the sector. This is the macro current your entire book is swimming against — AVGO, PLTR, CRWD, and QQQ are all feeling it directly.

headline: Micron sinks 6% in wild week
detail: Micron's sharp drop signals the chip sector is under real selling pressure, not just noise. SMH is your only winning position in chips — a continued semiconductor selloff could flip that green to red fast.

headline: U.S. confirms retaliatory strike on Iran, oil rises
detail: Geopolitical escalation in the Strait of Hormuz pushes oil higher and triggers risk-off moves in equities. For a margin account concentrated in tech, a sudden risk-off rotation is exactly the kind of move that turns manageable drawdowns into margin calls.

headline: SpaceX stock a 'terrible buy' — what it means for sentiment
detail: Negative press around SpaceX directly shadows SPCX and SPAL right out of the gate. SPAL is already down 17% and uses 2x leverage — bad sentiment plus leverage decay is a dangerous combo on a brand-new position.
